bullets.text: Full-year companywide CR of 87.4, well below the 96.0 target; Personal Lines CR was 87.5 and Commercial Lines CR was 87.0, improved 2.4 points YOY.
Companywide NPW grew 12% YOY; Personal Lines premiums reached $72.6 billion, up $9 billion (14%) driven primarily by personal auto at $66.0 billion.
Personal Lines PIFs grew 11% YOY to 37.4 million, adding 3.6 million policyholders in 2025; personal auto PIF growth was 13%; over 13.9 million PIFs added in the past five years.

Record capital generation year; debt-to-total capitalization ended below 20%; Board declared a $13.50/share annual-variable dividend; investment portfolio returned 7.3% (fixed income 7.0%, equity 16.8%).
Profitability benefited from continued lower personal auto frequency, favorable prior-year reserve development, and absence of significant catastrophe events.

Progressive accrued $1.2 billion in Florida personal auto policyholder credits under a Florida excess profit statute reflecting rates that generated above-cap returns; selective rate decreases initiated in markets where margins allow.

Non-acquisition expense ratio for Personal Lines vehicle business down 0.2 points YOY; LAE ratio declined 0.2 points driven by lower frequency, higher average premiums, and technology-driven productivity gains.

Progressive ranked in the 99th percentile of Gallup-surveyed companies for a fifth consecutive year; record 86% employee survey participation; voluntary turnover of 6.8% (excluding retirements); nearly 18,000 employees donated 132,000 volunteer hours.

BOP available in 46 states (80% of commercial multi-peril market); AutoQuote Explorer expanded to 11 carriers across 40 states; personal property Blueprint strategy targeting sustainable profitability in non-catastrophe-prone markets.

Advertising expense increased $1.1 billion YOY to fuel growth; AI-generated TV ad and YouTube/social campaigns (Taylor Swift album tie-in) drove strong brand metrics; NASCAR sponsorship with Denny Hamlin expanded reach.
